<file>
00001| """
00002| Real-time Regional AQI Integration
00003| Fetches live AQI data from WAQI (World Air Quality Index)
00004| Works for ANY city in India without physical sensors!
00005| """
00006| 
00007| import requests
00008| import time
00009| from datetime import datetime
00010| 
00011| # ==================== CONFIGURATION ====================
00012| 
00013| # Your WAQI API Token (get from: https://aqicn.org/data-platform/token/)
00014| WAQI_API_TOKEN = "465b3b09f08acc38dbe25719ea686a48d950c735"  # Replace with your actual token
00015| 
00016| # Your city name
00017| CITY = "banglore"  # Can be: hosur, bangalore, delhi, mumbai, etc.
00018| 
00019| # Backend URL
00020| BACKEND_URL = "http://localhost:5000"
00021| 
00022| # Update interval (seconds)
00023| UPDATE_INTERVAL = 300  # 5 minutes (to stay within free tier limits)
00024| 
00025| # ==================== WAQI API FUNCTIONS ====================
00026| 
00027| def get_city_aqi(city_name):
00028|     """
00029|     Fetch real-time AQI data for a specific city
00030|     API Documentation: https://aqicn.org/json-api/doc/
00031|     
00032|     Returns:
00033|     {
00034|         "aqi": 85,
00035|         "dominant_pollutant": "pm25",
00036|         "timestamp": "2026-01-08T13:00:00",
00037|         "city": "Hosur, India",
00038|         "pollutants": {...}
00039|     }
00040|     """
00041|     url = f"http://api.waqi.info/feed/{city_name}/?token={WAQI_API_TOKEN}"
00042|     
00043|     try:
00044|         response = requests.get(url, timeout=10)
00045|         data = response.json()
00046|         
00047|         if data['status'] == 'ok':
00048|             aqi_data = data['data']
00049|             
00050|             return {
00051|                 "aqi": aqi_data.get('aqi', 0),
00052|                 "dominant_pollutant": aqi_data.get('dominentpol', 'unknown'),
00053|                 "timestamp": aqi_data['time']['iso'],
00054|                 "city": aqi_data['city']['name'],
00055|                 "pollutants": {
00056|                     "pm25": aqi_data.get('iaqi', {}).get('pm25', {}).get('v', None),
00057|                     "pm10": aqi_data.get('iaqi', {}).get('pm10', {}).get('v', None),
00058|                     "o3": aqi_data.get('iaqi', {}).get('o3', {}).get('v', None),
00059|                     "no2": aqi_data.get('iaqi', {}).get('no2', {}).get('v', None),
00060|                     "co": aqi_data.get('iaqi', {}).get('co', {}).get('v', None)
00061|                 }
00062|             }
00063|         else:
00064|             print(f"❌ API Error: {data.get('data', 'Unknown error')}")
00065|             return None
00066|             
00067|     except Exception as e:
00068|         print(f"❌ Error fetching AQI: {e}")
00069|         return None
00070| 
00071| def get_nearby_aqi():
00072|     """
00073|     Get AQI based on your IP location (automatic)
00074|     Useful if city name doesn't work
00075|     """
00076|     url = f"http://api.waqi.info/feed/here/?token={WAQI_API_TOKEN}"
00077|     
00078|     try:
00079|         response = requests.get(url, timeout=10)
00080|         data = response.json()
00081|         
00082|         if data['status'] == 'ok':
00083|             return data['data']
00084|         else:
00085|             return None
00086|             
00087|     except Exception as e:
00088|         print(f"❌ Error: {e}")
00089|         return None
00090| 
00091| def get_coordinates_aqi(lat, lon):
00092|     """
00093|     Get AQI for specific GPS coordinates
00094|     Example: get_coordinates_aqi(12.7342, 77.8294)  # Hosur coordinates
00095|     """
00096|     url = f"http://api.waqi.info/feed/geo:{lat};{lon}/?token={WAQI_API_TOKEN}"
00097|     
00098|     try:
00099|         response = requests.get(url, timeout=10)
00100|         data = response.json()
00101|         
00102|         if data['status'] == 'ok':
00103|             return data['data']
00104|         else:
00105|             return None
00106|             
00107|     except Exception as e:
00108|         print(f"❌ Error: {e}")
00109|         return None
00110| 
00111| # ==================== SEND TO BACKEND ====================
00112| 
00113| def send_outdoor_aqi(aqi_value):
00114|     """
00115|     Send outdoor AQI data to Flask backend
00116|     """
00117|     data = {
00118|         "location_type": "outdoor",
00119|         "aqi_value": aqi_value,
00120|         "timestamp": datetime.now().isoformat()
00121|     }
00122|     
00123|     try:
00124|         response = requests.post(f"{BACKEND_URL}/api/aqi/add", json=data)
00125|         if response.status_code == 201:
00126|             print(f"✅ Outdoor AQI sent: {aqi_value}")
00127|             return True
00128|         else:
00129|             print(f"❌ Failed to send outdoor AQI: {response.status_code}")
00130|             return False
00131|     except Exception as e:
00132|         print(f"❌ Error sending data: {e}")
00133|         return False
00134| 
00135| def send_indoor_aqi(outdoor_aqi):
00136|     """
00137|     Estimate indoor AQI (typically 30-50% better than outdoor)
00138|     """
00139|     # Indoor AQI is usually better due to filtration
00140|     indoor_aqi = int(outdoor_aqi * 0.6)  # 40% reduction from outdoor
00141|     
00142|     data = {
00143|         "location_type": "indoor",
00144|         "aqi_value": indoor_aqi,
00145|         "timestamp": datetime.now().isoformat()
00146|     }
00147|     
00148|     try:
00149|         response = requests.post(f"{BACKEND_URL}/api/aqi/add", json=data)
00150|         if response.status_code == 201:
00151|             print(f"✅ Indoor AQI sent: {indoor_aqi}")
00152|             return True
00153|         else:
00154|             print(f"❌ Failed to send indoor AQI: {response.status_code}")
00155|             return False
00156|     except Exception as e:
00157|         print(f"❌ Error sending data: {e}")
00158|         return False
00159| 
00160| # ==================== MAIN LOOP ====================
00161| 
00162| def main():
00163|     """
00164|     Continuously fetch real-time AQI data and send to backend
00165|     """
00166|     print("\n" + "=" * 60)
00167|     print("  🌍 LIVE REGIONAL AQI INTEGRATION")
00168|     print("=" * 60)
00169|     print(f"  City: {CITY.upper()}")
00170|     print(f"  Update Interval: {UPDATE_INTERVAL} seconds")
00171|     print(f"  Backend: {BACKEND_URL}")
00172|     print("=" * 60 + "\n")
00173|     
00174|     # Test API connection
00175|     print("🔍 Testing API connection...")
00176|     test_data = get_city_aqi(CITY)
00177|     
00178|     if test_data:
00179|         print(f"✅ API Connected! Current AQI: {test_data['aqi']}")
00180|         print(f"   City: {test_data['city']}")
00181|         print(f"   Dominant Pollutant: {test_data['dominant_pollutant']}")
00182|     else:
00183|         print("❌ Failed to connect to API. Check your token and city name.")
00184|         print("\nTrying auto-location based on IP...")
00185|         test_data = get_nearby_aqi()
00186|         if test_data:
00187|             print(f"✅ Auto-location worked! AQI: {test_data.get('aqi')}")
00188|             print(f"   Location: {test_data['city']['name']}")
00189|         else:
00190|             print("❌ Auto-location also failed. Please check your API token.")
00191|             return
00192|     
00193|     print("\n🔄 Starting continuous monitoring...\n")
00194|     
00195|     iteration = 0
00196|     
00197|     try:
00198|         while True:
00199|             iteration += 1
00200|             print(f"\n⏱️  Update #{iteration} - {datetime.now().strftime('%I:%M:%S %p')}")
00201|             print("-" * 60)
00202|             
00203|             # Fetch real-time AQI
00204|             aqi_data = get_city_aqi(CITY)
00205|             
00206|             if aqi_data:
00207|                 outdoor_aqi = aqi_data['aqi']
00208|                 
00209|                 print(f"🌍 {aqi_data['city']}")
00210|                 print(f"   Outdoor AQI: {outdoor_aqi}")
00211|                 print(f"   Dominant Pollutant: {aqi_data['dominant_pollutant'].upper()}")
00212|                 
00213|                 # Show pollutant breakdown
00214|                 if aqi_data['pollutants']['pm25']:
00215|                     print(f"   PM2.5: {aqi_data['pollutants']['pm25']}")
00216|                 if aqi_data['pollutants']['pm10']:
00217|                     print(f"   PM10: {aqi_data['pollutants']['pm10']}")
00218|                 
00219|                 # Send to backend
00220|                 send_outdoor_aqi(outdoor_aqi)
00221|                 send_indoor_aqi(outdoor_aqi)
00222|                 
00223|             else:
00224|                 print("⚠️  Failed to fetch AQI data this cycle")
00225|             
00226|             print(f"\n⏳ Next update in {UPDATE_INTERVAL} seconds...")
00227|             print("-" * 60)
00228|             time.sleep(UPDATE_INTERVAL)
00229|             
00230|     except KeyboardInterrupt:
00231|         print("\n\n🛑 Stopping AQI monitoring...")
00232|         print("Goodbye! 👋\n")
00233| 
00234| if __name__ == "__main__":
00235|     main()
00236|

metadata.preview:
"""
Real-time Regional AQI Integration
Fetches live AQI data from WAQI (World Air Quality Index)
Works for ANY city in India without physical sensors!
"""

import requests
import time
from datetime import datetime

# ==================== CONFIGURATION ====================

# Your WAQI API Token (get from: https://aqicn.org/data-platform/token/)
WAQI_API_TOKEN = "465b3b09f08acc38dbe25719ea686a48d950c735"  # Replace with your actual token

# Your city name
CITY = "banglore"  # Can be: hosur, bangalore, delhi, mumbai, etc.

# Backend URL
BACKEND_URL = "http://localhost:5000"
